Total Participants - N.evidence: The Results section states: 'Between Nov 27, 2013, and Dec 20, 2018, 1173 patients were enrolled (one patient subsequently withdrew consent for analysis of his data) and assigned to receive SOC (n=296), SOC plus radiotherapy (n=293), SOC plus abiraterone (n=292), or SOC plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone (n=291; figure 1).' It is further clarified in multiple places (e.g., Table 1, Table 2, and the flowchart) that the overall population for efficacy analysis is n=1172, which excludes the one patient who withdrew consent. This number includes all participants randomized to either treatment or control arms.

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.value: second-generation androgen signalling inhibitor (abiraterone, combined with prednisone) plus androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) with or without docetaxel
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.evidence: The methods and summary sections state that the first experimental arm (SOC plus abiraterone) consisted of abiraterone (a second-generation androgen signalling inhibitor) plus prednisone, added to standard of care (SOC), which was ADT with or without docetaxel. The study design and multiple tables (e.g., Table 1) confirm that the treatment arm included abiraterone plus prednisone in addition to ADT (and, depending on protocol amendment, with or without docetaxel).
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1

Treatment Arm(s).value: standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: The methods section states: 'Participants were randomly assigned (1:1:1:1) to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone.' This is confirmed in the trial profile and throughout the results and tables.

Number of Arms Included.evidence: The Methods section and the trial profile (Figure 1) state that participants were randomly assigned (1:1:1:1) to one of four groups: standard of care (SOC), SOC plus radiotherapy, SOC plus abiraterone, or SOC plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone. This confirms that the trial included a total of four arms (two experimental: SOC plus abiraterone, SOC plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone; and two control: SOC, SOC plus radiotherapy).

Treatment Arm - N.value: 292 (SOC plus abiraterone), 291 (SOC plus abiraterone plus radiotherapy)
Treatment Arm - N.evidence: In the Results section and Figure 1 (Trial profile), it is stated that 292 patients were assigned to SOC plus abiraterone and 291 to SOC plus abiraterone plus radiotherapy. These are the experimental arms with abiraterone. Table 1 and Table 2 confirm these numbers, and the text consistently refers to these two groups as the abiraterone arms. The total number of participants in the experimental arms is thus 292 and 291, reported separately as per instructions.

Control Arm - N.evidence: The control arm is defined as 'SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy)'. Table 1 and multiple sections (e.g., flowchart, Table 2, Figure 2) consistently report 589 patients in the control arm for the overall population and 355 patients in the control arm for the ADT with docetaxel population. These numbers are confirmed in the flowchart and efficacy tables.

Control Arm.value: standard of care (SOC) (androgen deprivation therapy [ADT] alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), with or without radiotherapy
Control Arm.evidence: The control arm is described in the Methods section: 'Participants were randomly assigned (1:1:1:1) to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone..., or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone.' The Results and Table 1 further clarify that the SOC arm includes ADT alone or with docetaxel, with or without radiotherapy, and that abiraterone is the experimental addition. The pooled control group for efficacy analyses is referred to as 'SOC without abiraterone (with or without radiotherapy)'.

Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.value: Standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy with docetaxel) plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily), with or without radiotherapy
Treatment Arm 1 Regimen.evidence: The Methods section states that participants were randomly assigned to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone. The Results and Discussion clarify that the main experimental regimen (treatment arm 1) is standard of care (ADT with docetaxel) plus abiraterone and prednisone, with or without radiotherapy. The regimen details (dosing and schedule) are specified in the Procedures section.

Quality of Life reported.evidence: Quality of life is listed as a secondary endpoint in the Outcomes section, but the text explicitly states that 'Apart from CRPC-free survival, prostate-cancer-specific survival, event rate per 100 person-years, and toxicity, secondary endpoint results were still under investigation at the time this manuscript was submitted and so are not reported here.' There are no results, tables, or figures reporting quality of life outcomes in the document.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.evidence: The median abiraterone treatment duration before discontinuation in the ADT with docetaxel population (i.e., the treatment arm) is reported as 34.1 months (95% CI 30.0-43.5) in the Results section: 'Overall, the median abiraterone treatment duration before discontinuation was 33.2 months (95% CI 25.5-43.2) in the ADT without docetaxel population and 34.1 months (30.0-43.5) in the ADT with docetaxel population.'

Trial.evidence: The name of the clinical study is stated multiple times in the document, including in the article title ('Abiraterone plus prednisone added to androgen deprivation therapy and docetaxel in de novo metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer (PEACE-1): a multicentre, open-label, randomised, phase 3 study with a 2 x 2 factorial design') and in the methods section ('We conducted an open-label, randomised, phase 3 study with a 2x2 factorial design (PEACE-1)').

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Treat.evidence: Table 1 (Baseline characteristics in the intention-to-treat population) under 'ADT with docetaxel population' shows 'Metastatic burden: High burden' as 224 (63%) for 'SOC plus abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=355)', which is the treatment arm.

Volume of disease - N (%) | High | Contr.evidence: Table 1 (Baseline characteristics in the intention-to-treat population) under 'ADT with docetaxel population' shows 'Metastatic burden: High burden' as 232 (65%) for 'SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=355)', which is the control arm.

Volume of disease - N (%) | Low | Contro.evidence: Table 1 (Baseline characteristics in the intention-to-treat population) under 'ADT with docetaxel population' shows 'Metastatic burden: Low burden' as 123 (35%) for 'SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=355)', which is the control arm.

Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).value: 3.5 years (IQR 2.8–4.6) for radiographic progression-free survival and 4.4 years (3.5–5.4) for overall survival
Median Follow-Up Duration (mo).evidence: In the 'Findings' section and again in the 'Results' section, the median follow-up for the whole population is reported as 3.5 years (IQR 2.8–4.6) for radiographic progression-free survival and 4.4 years (3.5–5.4) for overall survival. Since 1 year = 12 months, this corresponds to 42 months (IQR 33.6–55.2) for radiographic progression-free survival and 52.8 months (42–64.8) for overall survival. The values are given in years in the text, not in months, so the answer is provided as reported in the document.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Synchronous.evidence: The trial exclusively enrolled patients with de novo (synchronous) metastatic prostate cancer, but the exact number and percentage of synchronous metastases in each arm (treatment or control) is not explicitly reported in any table or text. All patients are de novo, but no arm-wise breakdown is given.

The trial population is described as de novo (synchronous) metastatic prostate cancer, but the number and percentage of synchronous metastases in the control arm is not explicitly reported in any table or text. All patients are de novo, but no arm-wise breakdown is given.

Mode of metastases - N (%) | Metachronou.evidence: The study only included patients with de novo (synchronous) metastatic prostate cancer. No patients with metachronous metastases were enrolled, and no numbers or percentages are reported for metachronous metastases in any arm.

The study only included patients with de novo (synchronous) metastatic prostate cancer. No patients with metachronous metastases were enrolled, and no numbers or percentages are reported for metachronous metastases in any arm.

Quality of Life Scale.evidence: The document describes quality of life as a secondary endpoint and mentions that results for this endpoint were still under investigation at the time the manuscript was submitted and are not reported here. No data or results for a quality of life scale or questionnaire are provided in the main text, tables, or figures.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: In the ADT with docetaxel population (the population of interest for the treatment group), Table 1 and the flowchart (Figure 1) show that 355 patients in the SOC plus abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy) were included, and all 355 received docetaxel. This is confirmed in Table 1 under 'ADT with docetaxel population' and in the text: '355 included in the ADT with docetaxel population for efficacy analysis' for both abiraterone and non-abiraterone groups. Therefore, 100% of the treatment arm received docetaxel.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: In the ADT with docetaxel population (the population of interest for the control group), Table 1 and the flowchart (Figure 1) show that 355 patients in the SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy) were included, and all 355 received docetaxel. This is confirmed in Table 1 under 'ADT with docetaxel population' and in the text: '355 included in the ADT with docetaxel population for efficacy analysis' for both abiraterone and non-abiraterone groups. Therefore, 100% of the control arm received docetaxel.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: The study population is exclusively de novo metastatic (synchronous) prostate cancer, as stated in the methods and throughout the document (e.g., 'de novo metastatic castration-sensitive prostate cancer' in summary and methods). All results are reported for this synchronous group.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: The study explicitly states in the discussion: 'PEACE-1 only included patients with de novo mCSPC, it is still unclear whether this triple therapy might benefit patients with metachronous mCSPC.' No results are reported for metachronous disease.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: Results are reported by high-volume metastatic burden, as shown in Table 1 (baseline characteristics), Table 2 (efficacy outcomes), and Figure 2F (overall survival in high-volume group), as well as in the text (e.g., 'By analysing the ADT with docetaxel population according to the disease burden...high metastatic burden...').

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | L.evidence: Results are reported by low-volume metastatic burden, as shown in Table 1 (baseline characteristics), Table 2 (efficacy outcomes), and Figure 2E (overall survival in low-volume group), as well as in the text (e.g., 'By analysing the ADT with docetaxel population according to the disease burden...low metastatic burden...').

Trial Name.evidence: The trial name is given in the title and throughout the document as 'PEACE-1'.

Author.evidence: The author list is provided at the beginning of the article, with Karim Fizazi listed first, followed by many others, and 'on behalf of the PEACE-1 investigators'. The 'et al.' format is appropriate.

Year.evidence: The publication date is stated as 'Published Online April 8, 2022' and 'Copyright © 2022 Elsevier Ltd.'

Full Pub or Abstract.evidence: The document is a full publication from The Lancet, not just an abstract, as evidenced by the detailed methods, results, and discussion sections.

Phase.evidence: The study is described multiple times as a 'phase 3 study' (e.g., in the title and methods section).

Original/Follow Up.evidence: The document describes the original report of the PEACE-1 trial's primary and secondary outcomes, not a follow-up analysis.

Primary Endpoint(s).value: The coprimary endpoints were radiographic progression-free survival and overall survival.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.value: HR 0.54 (99.9% CI 0.41–0.71); median PFS 4.5 years (SOC plus abiraterone) vs 2.2 years (SOC without abiraterone)
PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The overall population results for radiographic progression-free survival (PFS) are reported in multiple locations, including the summary, results, and Table 2. The hazard ratio (HR) for PFS in the treatment arm (SOC plus abiraterone, with or without radiotherapy) is 0.54 (99.9% CI 0.41–0.71), with a median PFS of 4.5 years for the treatment group. No explicit PFS rate (%) at a specific timepoint is given, but the HR and median PFS are the main reported summary statistics for the whole population.

PFS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: The control arm is SOC without abiraterone (with or without radiotherapy). The HR is reported as 1 (reference), and the median PFS is 2.2 years. No explicit PFS rate (%) at a specific timepoint is given for the control group, but the median PFS and HR (relative to control) are the main reported summary statistics for the whole population.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.evidence: The document provides detailed results on efficacy and safety outcomes, statistical methods, and subgroup analyses for the randomized controlled trial, but does not report an explicit overall risk judgment (risk of bias or quality assessment) for individual criteria of evidence (COE) in the RCT. There is no section or table summarizing risk of bias or overall risk judgment for the trial or its outcomes.

Treatment Class.value: androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) with or without docetaxel, with or without abiraterone plus prednisone, with or without radiotherapy
Treatment Class.evidence: The document describes a 2x2 factorial randomized trial in which participants were assigned to one of four groups: standard of care (SOC, defined as ADT alone or with docetaxel), SOC plus radiotherapy, SOC plus abiraterone (abiraterone plus prednisone), or SOC plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone. The SOC evolved during the trial to include ADT with docetaxel as the main comparator. Thus, the treatment classes received by participants were combinations of ADT, docetaxel, abiraterone plus prednisone, and radiotherapy. This is confirmed in the Methods, Results, and Table 1, which detail the arms and treatments received.

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.evidence: Table 3 (page with id '10-1') reports for the 'ADT with docetaxel population' (the main population of interest per the summary and methods): 'Severe (grade ≥3) adverse events' occurred in 217 (63%) of 347 patients in the 'SOC plus abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy)' (treatment arm).

Table 3 (page with id '10-1') reports for the 'ADT with docetaxel population': 'Severe (grade ≥3) adverse events' occurred in 181 (52%) of 350 patients in the 'SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy)' (control arm).

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: The document and Table 3 report 'severe (grade ≥3) adverse events' but do not specify if these are treatment-related or all-cause. No explicit data for 'treatment-related' grade 3 or higher adverse events is provided.

The document and Table 3 report 'severe (grade ≥3) adverse events' but do not specify if these are treatment-related or all-cause. No explicit data for 'treatment-related' grade 3 or higher adverse events is provided.

Table 3 (page with id '10-1') reports for the 'ADT with docetaxel population': 'Fatal (grade 5) adverse events' occurred in 7 (2%) of 347 patients in the 'SOC plus abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy)' (treatment arm). The table does not specify if these are treatment-related, but the context and standard reporting suggest these are all-cause unless otherwise specified.

Table 3 (page with id '10-1') reports for the 'ADT with docetaxel population': 'Fatal (grade 5) adverse events' occurred in 3 (1%) of 350 patients in the 'SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy)' (control arm). The table does not specify if these are treatment-related, but the context and standard reporting suggest these are all-cause unless otherwise specified.

Add-on Treatment.value: abiraterone plus prednisone (with or without radiotherapy)
Add-on Treatment.evidence: The document describes a 2x2 factorial trial design where the add-on treatment to standard of care (SOC, defined as androgen deprivation therapy [ADT] with or without docetaxel) was abiraterone plus prednisone, with or without radiotherapy. This is explicitly stated in the Methods section: 'Participants were randomly assigned (1:1:1:1) to standard of care (androgen deprivation therapy alone or with intravenous docetaxel 75 mg/m² once every 3 weeks), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone.' The main results and tables consistently refer to the add-on as 'abiraterone plus prednisone (with or without radiotherapy)'.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: Table 2 (Efficacy outcomes in the intention-to-treat population) reports 'Radiographic progression-free survival' for the overall population as 4.5 years for SOC plus abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=583).

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Control.evidence: Table 2 (Efficacy outcomes in the intention-to-treat population) reports 'Radiographic progression-free survival' for the overall population as 2.2 years for SOC without abiraterone groups (with or without radiotherapy; n=589).

Median PFS (mo) | High volume | Treatmen.evidence: Table 2 (Efficacy outcomes in the intention-to-treat population) reports 'Radiographic progression-free survival in patients with high-volume metastatic burden' in the ADT with docetaxel population as 4.1 years for SOC plus abiraterone groups (n=225).

Median PFS (mo) | High volume | Control.evidence: Table 2 (Efficacy outcomes in the intention-to-treat population) reports 'Radiographic progression-free survival in patients with high-volume metastatic burden' in the ADT with docetaxel population as 1.6 years for SOC without abiraterone groups (n=231).

Median PFS (mo) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: Table 2 (Efficacy outcomes in the intention-to-treat population) reports 'Radiographic progression-free survival in patients with low-volume metastatic burden' in the ADT with docetaxel population as 'NR' (not reached) for SOC plus abiraterone groups (n=129).

Median PFS (mo) | Low volume | Control.evidence: Table 2 (Efficacy outcomes in the intention-to-treat population) reports 'Radiographic progression-free survival in patients with low-volume metastatic burden' in the ADT with docetaxel population as 2.7 years for SOC without abiraterone groups (n=122).

Type of Therapy.value: androgen deprivation therapy (ADT) with or without docetaxel, with or without radiotherapy, with or without abiraterone plus prednisone
Type of Therapy.evidence: The Methods and Results sections, as well as the trial profile and Table 1, describe that participants were randomized to receive standard of care (ADT alone or with docetaxel), standard of care plus radiotherapy, standard of care plus abiraterone (oral 1000 mg abiraterone once daily plus oral 5 mg prednisone twice daily), or standard of care plus radiotherapy plus abiraterone. The study used a 2x2 factorial design, so the type of therapy for the whole population includes all these combinations.

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: No explicit overall survival rate (percentage) at latest follow-up is reported for the experimental (abiraterone) arm in the overall population. Only median overall survival (5.7 years vs 4.7 years) and hazard ratios are provided (Table 2, Figure 2C).

OS Rate (%) | Overall | Control.evidence: No explicit overall survival rate (percentage) at latest follow-up is reported for the control arm in the overall population. Only median overall survival (4.7 years) and hazard ratios are provided (Table 2, Figure 2C).

OS Rate (%) | Synchronous | Treatment.evidence: All patients in PEACE-1 had de novo (synchronous) metastatic disease, but no explicit OS rate (%) is reported for this subgroup; only medians and HRs are given.

OS Rate (%) | Synchronous | Control.evidence: All patients had synchronous metastasis, but no explicit OS rate (%) is reported for this subgroup; only medians and HRs are given.

OS Rate (%) | Metachronous | Treatment.evidence: The study only included de novo (synchronous) metastatic patients; metachronous subgroup is not present.

OS Rate (%) | Metachronous | Control.evidence: The study only included de novo (synchronous) metastatic patients; metachronous subgroup is not present.

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Treatment.evidence: For high-volume disease in the ADT+docetaxel population, only median OS (5.1 years vs 3.5 years) and HR (0.72) are reported (Table 2, Figure 2F); no OS rate (%) at a specific timepoint is given.

OS Rate (%) | High volume | Control.evidence: For high-volume disease in the ADT+docetaxel population, only median OS (3.5 years) and HR (0.72) are reported (Table 2, Figure 2F); no OS rate (%) at a specific timepoint is given.

OS Rate (%) | Low volume | Treatment.evidence: For low-volume disease in the ADT+docetaxel population, only median OS (not reached) and HR (0.83) are reported (Table 2, Figure 2E); no OS rate (%) at a specific timepoint is given.

OS Rate (%) | Low volume | Control.evidence: For low-volume disease in the ADT+docetaxel population, only median OS (not reached) and HR (0.83) are reported (Table 2, Figure 2E); no OS rate (%) at a specific timepoint is given.
